description_long: This bungalow features a long, rectangular brick structure with a prominent gable roof that extends over the main living area and a secondary section, likely a garage or utility space. The roof is clad in dark gray shingles and includes two visible brick chimneys, adding to its residential character. The exterior walls are constructed from reddish-brown brick, and the windows are arranged in a linear pattern along the longer side, providing ample natural light. The front facade is simple, with a single door and a few windows, while the rear and side elevations show additional windows and a covered entryway. The building sits low to the ground, typical of bungalow designs, and lacks any visible porch or veranda. The overall style is practical and unadorned, suitable for suburban or urban neighborhoods.
description: A long, brick bungalow with a dark gable roof, two chimneys, and linear window arrangements. The structure is low-profile and practical, with no visible porch.
description_view_0: Side view showing the elongated brick facade, multiple windows, and the main gable roof with two chimneys.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the gable end, a single window, and the main entrance beneath the roof peak.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the full length of the building, the secondary roof section, and additional windows.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the back gable, minimal window openings, and the extension of the roofline.
